title: Magnet Compass Explorer

additionalProps.title: 🧲 Magnet Explorer
additionalProps.subtitle: Discover the North-Seeking Pole!

feedbackMessages.correct: 🌍 The red (N) end is the North-seeking pole because it always points toward geographic North. This happens because the Earth itself behaves like a giant magnet! Its geographic North is near Earth's magnetic South — so opposite poles attract, pulling the magnet's North-seeking pole toward geographic North.
feedbackMessages.incorrect: 💡 Remember: The end that points toward geographic North (toward the 'N' on the compass rose) is the North-seeking pole. The compass rose shows you the direction!

additionalProps.hintText: 🌍 The Earth acts like a giant magnet! The end of a magnet that points toward geographic North is called the North-seeking pole. The red (N) end of our magnet is pointing toward geographic North on the compass rose. So... 🤔

instructions_for_student: Drag the magnet to the thread hanging from the wooden stand. Watch it swing and settle along the north–south direction. Then label each end: the end pointing to the compass rose 'N' is the North-seeking pole, and the end pointing to 'S' is the South-seeking pole. Press 'Check My Answer!' to see if you are correct!
learning_objectives: Understand that a freely suspended magnet always rests along the north–south direction
Identify the North-seeking pole and South-seeking pole of a bar magnet
Understand that the Earth itself behaves like a giant magnet
Connect the concept of magnetic compass to direction finding

agent_notes.tool_description: Interactive drag-and-drop simulation where Grade 6 students suspend a bar magnet from a thread, observe it settling along north–south, and label the poles using dropdowns. Feedback explains the Earth-as-magnet principle.
phases: idle — magnet rests on table; student drags it
swinging — magnet swings with CSS animation after release near thread
settled — magnet rests horizontally; compass rose is animated; label UI appears
labeling — student selects labels from dropdowns
feedback — correctness panel with explanation and star reward

customisation_via_additionalProps.showHint: boolean — toggles the hint button (default true)
customisation_via_additionalProps.title: string — header title text
customisation_via_additionalProps.subtitle: string — subtitle below header
customisation_via_additionalProps.compassSize: number — diameter of compass rose in px (default 120)
customisation_via_additionalProps.magnetColor.northHalf: CSS color string for N half of magnet
customisation_via_additionalProps.magnetColor.southHalf: CSS color string for S half of magnet
